title: Warm Buttery Vietnamese Sauerkraut Banh Cuon
description: A light and refreshing Vietnamese dish featuring white sauerkraut and double-rise bread, wrapped in a lotus leaf and served warm with a buttery sauce.

ingredients.name: double-rise bread
ingredients.notes: toasted and cut into small pieces

instructions: In a small saucepan, melt the butter over low heat and stir in the fish sauce, lime juice, and grated ginger until well combined.
In a large bowl, combine the toasted bread, sauerkraut, and melted butter mixture, and toss until the bread is evenly coated.
Season the mixture with salt and black pepper to taste.
Lay a lotus leaf flat on a work surface and place about 1/2 cup of the bread and sauerkraut mixture in the center of the leaf.
Fold the bottom half of the leaf over the filling, then fold in the sides and roll the leaf into a tight cylinder.
Repeat with the remaining ingredients to make 3 more rolls.
Serve the banh cuon warm, with additional butter and fish sauce on the side if desired.
To cook the banh cuon, steam them over boiling water for 5 minutes, or until the leaves are tender and the filling is heated through.
To serve, slice each roll in half and garnish with additional grated ginger and lime wedges if desired.
